001/*
002 * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more
003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.microservice;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.CollectionUtils.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.FileUtils.*;
021import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.IoUtils.*;
022import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.ObjectUtils.*;
023import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Shorts.*;
024import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.ThrowableUtils.*;
025
026import java.io.*;
027import java.nio.file.*;
028import java.text.*;
029import java.util.*;
030import java.util.concurrent.*;
031import java.util.concurrent.atomic.*;
032import java.util.jar.*;
033import java.util.logging.*;
034
035import org.apache.juneau.commons.inject.*;
036import org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect.*;
037import org.apache.juneau.commons.runtime.*;
038import org.apache.juneau.commons.settings.*;
039import org.apache.juneau.commons.svl.*;
040import org.apache.juneau.commons.svl.vars.*;
041import org.apache.juneau.config.*;
042import org.apache.juneau.config.event.*;
043import org.apache.juneau.config.store.*;
044import org.apache.juneau.config.store.FileStore;
045import org.apache.juneau.config.vars.*;
046import org.apache.juneau.marshall.collections.*;
047import org.apache.juneau.marshall.cp.*;
048import org.apache.juneau.marshall.parser.ParseException;
049import org.apache.juneau.microservice.console.*;
050import org.apache.juneau.microservice.resources.*;
051
052/**
053 * Parent class for all microservices.
054 *
055 * <p>
056 * A microservice defines a simple API for starting and stopping simple Java services contained in executable jars.
057 *
058 * <p>
059 * The general command for creating and starting a microservice from a main method is as follows:
060 * <p class='bjava'>
061 *    <jk>public static void</jk> main(String[] <jv>args</jv>) {
062 *       Microservice.<jsm>create</jsm>().args(<jv>args</jv>).build().start().join();
063 *  }
064 * </p>
065 *
066 * <p>
067 * Your microservice class must be specified as the <jk>Main-Class</jk> entry in the manifest file of your microservice
068 * jar file if it's an executable jar.
069 *
070 * <h5 class='topic'>Microservice Configuration</h5>
071 *
072 * This class defines the following method for accessing configuration for your microservice:
073 * <ul class='spaced-list'>
074 *    <li>
075 *       {@link #getArgs()} - The command-line arguments passed to the jar file.
076 *    <li>
077 *       {@link #getConfig()} - An external INI-style configuration file.
078 *    <li>
079 *       {@link #getManifest()} - The manifest file for the main jar file.
080 * </ul>
081 *
082 * <h5 class='topic'>Lifecycle Methods</h5>
083 *
084 * Subclasses must implement the following lifecycle methods:
085 * <ul class='spaced-list'>
086 *    <li>
087 *       {@link #init()} - Gets executed immediately following construction.
088 *    <li>
089 *       {@link #start()} - Gets executed during startup.
090 *    <li>
091 *       {@link #stop()} - Gets executed when 'exit' is typed in the console or an external shutdown signal is received.
092 *    <li>
093 *       {@link #kill()} - Can be used to forcibly shut down the service.  Doesn't get called during normal operation.
094 * </ul>
095 *
096 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
097 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/JuneauMicroservice">juneau-microservice Basics</a>
098 * </ul>
099 */
100@SuppressWarnings({
101   "resource",  // consoleReader and consoleWriter are backed by system streams or caller-provided streams; lifecycle managed by stop()
102   "java:S3740" // Raw Class/Builder types used intentionally for reflective microservice wiring where type parameters are not available
103})
104public class Microservice implements ConfigEventListener {
105
106   /**
107    * Builder class.
108    */
109   @SuppressWarnings({
110      "resource" // configStore, consoleReader, consoleWriter are passed in by the caller or assigned to system streams; lifecycle managed externally
111   })
112   public static class Builder implements BeanStoreOverridable<Builder> {
113
114      Args args;
115      ManifestFile manifest;
116      Logger logger;
117      LogConfig logConfig;
118      Config config;
119      String configName;
120      ConfigStore configStore;
121      Config.Builder configBuilder = Config.create();
122      Boolean consoleEnabled;
123      List<ConsoleCommand> consoleCommands = list();
124      VarResolver.Builder varResolver = VarResolver.create().defaultVars().vars(ConfigVar.class);
125      Scanner consoleReader;
126      PrintWriter consoleWriter;
127      MicroserviceListener listener;
128      File workingDir;
129      WritableBeanStore beanStore;
130      BeanStore overridingBeanStore;
131      List<Class<?>> configurations = list();
132
133      /**
134       * Constructor.
135       */
136      protected Builder() {}
137
138      /**
139       * {@link Inject @Inject}-annotated initializer that populates {@link #workingDir} from the
140       * {@code juneau.workingDir} property (system property &rarr; environment variable &rarr;
141       * registered property sources) when the property is set. Has no effect if {@code workingDir}
142       * was already set programmatically.
143       *
144       * @param workingDirEnv The resolved {@code juneau.workingDir} value, or {@code null}/empty
145       *    when not configured.
146       */
147      @Inject
148      public void initWorkingDirFromEnv(@Value("${juneau.workingDir}") String workingDirEnv) {
149         if (workingDir == null && ine(workingDirEnv))
150            workingDir = new File(workingDirEnv);
151      }
152
153      /**
154       * Copy constructor.
155       *
156       * @param copyFrom The builder to copy.
157       */
158      protected Builder(Builder copyFrom) {
159         this.args = copyFrom.args;
160         this.manifest = copyFrom.manifest;
161         this.logger = copyFrom.logger;
162         this.configName = copyFrom.configName;
163         this.logConfig = copyFrom.logConfig == null ? null : copyFrom.logConfig.copy();
164         this.consoleEnabled = copyFrom.consoleEnabled;
165         this.configBuilder = copyFrom.configBuilder;
166         this.varResolver = copyFrom.varResolver;
167         this.consoleReader = copyFrom.consoleReader;
168         this.consoleWriter = copyFrom.consoleWriter;
169         this.workingDir = copyFrom.workingDir;
170         this.beanStore = copyFrom.beanStore;
171         this.overridingBeanStore = copyFrom.overridingBeanStore;
172         this.configurations = new ArrayList<>(copyFrom.configurations);
173      }
174
175      /**
176       * Specifies the command-line arguments passed into the Java command.
177       *
178       * <p>
179       * This is required if you use {@link Microservice#getArgs()} or <c>$A</c> string variables.
180       *
181       * @param args
182       *    The command-line arguments passed into the Java command as a pre-parsed {@link Args} object.
183       * @return This object.
184       */
185      public Builder args(Args args) {
186         this.args = args;
187         return this;
188      }
189
190      /**
191       * Specifies the command-line arguments passed into the Java command.
192       *
193       * <p>
194       * This is required if you use {@link Microservice#getArgs()} or <c>$A</c> string variables.
195       *
196       * @param args
197       *    The command-line arguments passed into the Java command as the raw command-line arguments.
198       * @return This object.
199       */
200      public Builder args(String...args) {
201         this.args = new Args(args);
202         return this;
203      }
204
205      /**
206       * Instantiate a new microservice using the settings defined on this builder.
207       *
208       * @return A new microservice.
209       * @throws Exception Error occurred.
210       */
211      @SuppressWarnings({
212         "java:S112" // throws Exception intentional - callback/lifecycle method
213      })
214      public Microservice build() throws Exception {
215         return new Microservice(this);
216      }
217
218      /**
219       * Specifies the config for initializing this microservice.
220       *
221       * <p>
222       * Calling this method overrides the default configuration controlled by the {@link #configName(String)} and {@link #configStore(ConfigStore)} methods.
223       *
224       * @param config The configuration.
225       * @return This object.
226       */
227      public Builder config(Config config) {
228         this.config = config;
229         return this;
230      }
231
232      /**
233       * Specifies the config name for initializing this microservice.
234       *
235       * <p>
236       * If you do not specify the config file location, we attempt to resolve it through the following methods:
237       * <ol class='spaced-list'>
238       *    <li>
239       *       Resolve file first in working directory, then in classpath, using the following names:
240       *       <ul>
241       *          <li>
242       *             The <js>"configFile"</js> argument in the command line arguments passed in through the constructor.
243       *          <li>
244       *             The value of the <c>Main-Config</c> entry in the manifest file.
245       *          <li>
246       *             A config file in the same location and with the same name as the executable jar file.
247       *             (e.g. <js>"java -jar myjar.jar"</js> will look for <js>"myjar.cfg"</js>).
248       *       </ul>
249       *    <li>
250       *       Resolve any <js>"*.cfg"</js> file that can be found in the working directory.
251       *    <li>
252       *       Resolve any of the following files in the classpath:  <js>"juneau.cfg"</js>, <js>"system.cfg"</js>
253       * </ol>
254       *
255       * <p>
256       * If no configuration file is found, and empty in-memory configuration is used.
257       *
258       * @param configName The configuration name.
259       * @return This object.
260       */
261      public Builder configName(String configName) {
262         this.configName = configName;
263         return this;
264      }
265
266      /**
267       * Specifies the config store to use for storing and retrieving configurations.
268       *
269       * <p>
270       * By default, we use a {@link FileStore} store for configuration files.
271       *
272       * @param configStore The configuration name.
273       * @return This object.
274       */
275      public Builder configStore(ConfigStore configStore) {
276         this.configStore = configStore;
277         return this;
278      }
279
280      /**
281       * Specifies the console input and output.
282       *
283       * <p>
284       * If not specified, uses the console returned by {@link System#console()}.
285       * If that is not available, uses {@link System#in} and {@link System#out}.
286       *
287       * <p>
288       * Note that these are ignored if the console is not enabled via {@link #consoleEnabled(boolean)}.
289       *
290       * @param consoleReader The console input.
291       * @param consoleWriter The console output.
292       * @return This object.
293       */
294      public Builder console(Scanner consoleReader, PrintWriter consoleWriter) {
295         this.consoleReader = consoleReader;
296         this.consoleWriter = consoleWriter;
297         return this;
298      }
299
300      /**
301       * Specifies console commands to make available on the Java console.
302       *
303       * <p>
304       * Note that these are ignored if the console is not enabled via {@link #consoleEnabled(boolean)}.
305       *
306       * <p>
307       * This list augments the commands defined via the <js>"Console/commands"</js> configuration setting.
308       *
309       * <p>
310       * This method can only be used on console commands with no-arg constructors.
311       *
312       * @param consoleCommands The list of console commands to append to the list of available commands.
313       * @return This object.
314       * @throws ExecutableException Exception occurred on invoked constructor/method/field.
315       */
316      @SuppressWarnings({
317         "unchecked" // Type erasure requires unchecked cast for varargs
318      })
319      public Builder consoleCommands(Class<? extends ConsoleCommand>...consoleCommands) throws ExecutableException {
320         try {
321            for (var cc : consoleCommands)
322               this.consoleCommands.add(cc.getDeclaredConstructor().newInstance());
323         } catch (Exception e) {
324            throw new ExecutableException(e);
325         }
326         return this;
327      }
328
329      /**
330       * Specifies console commands to make available on the Java console.
331       *
332       * <p>
333       * Note that these are ignored if the console is not enabled via {@link #consoleEnabled(boolean)}.
334       *
335       * <p>
336       * This list augments the commands defined via the <js>"Console/commands"</js> configuration setting.
337       *
338       * @param consoleCommands The list of console commands to append to the list of available commands.
339       * @return This object.
340       */
341      public Builder consoleCommands(ConsoleCommand...consoleCommands) {
342         addAll(this.consoleCommands, consoleCommands);
343         return this;
344      }
345
346      /**
347       * Specifies that the Java console is enabled for this microservice.
348       *
349       * <p>
350       * If not specified, this value is taken from the <js>"Console/enabled"</js> configuration setting.
351       * If not specified in the configuration, defaults to <jk>false</jk>.
352       *
353       * @param consoleEnabled <jk>true</jk> if the Java console is enabled for this microservice.
354       * @return This object.
355       */
356      public Builder consoleEnabled(boolean consoleEnabled) {
357         this.consoleEnabled = consoleEnabled;
358         return this;
359      }
360
361      /**
362       * Creates a copy of this builder.
363       *
364       * @return A new copy of this builder.
365       */
366      public Builder copy() {
367         return new Builder(this);
368      }
369
370      /**
371       * Registers an event listener for this microservice.
372       *
373       * @param listener An event listener for this microservice.
374       * @return This object.
375       */
376      public Builder listener(MicroserviceListener listener) {
377         this.listener = listener;
378         return this;
379      }
380
381      /**
382       * Specifies logging instructions for the microservice.
383       *
384       * <p>
385       * If not specified, the values are taken from the <js>"Logging"</js> section of the configuration.
386       *
387       * <p>
388       * This method is ignored if {@link #logger(Logger)} is used to set the microservice logger.
389       *
390       * @param logConfig The log configuration.
391       * @return This object.
392       */
393      public Builder logConfig(LogConfig logConfig) {
394         this.logConfig = logConfig;
395         return this;
396      }
397
398      /**
399       * Specifies the logger used by the microservice and returned by the {@link Microservice#getLogger()} method.
400       *
401       * <p>
402       * Calling this method overrides the default logging mechanism controlled by the {@link #logConfig(LogConfig)} method.
403       *
404       * @param logger The logger to use for logging microservice messages.
405       * @return This object.
406       */
407      public Builder logger(Logger logger) {
408         this.logger = logger;
409         return this;
410      }
411
412      /**
413       * Specifies the manifest file of the jar file this microservice is contained within.
414       *
415       * <p>
416       * This is required if you use {@link Microservice#getManifest()}.
417       * It's also used to locate initialization values such as <c>Main-Config</c>.
418       *
419       * <p>
420       * If you do not specify the manifest file, we attempt to resolve it through the following methods:
421       * <ol class='spaced-list'>
422       *    <li>
423       *       Looking on the file system for a file at <js>"META-INF/MANIFEST.MF"</js>.
424       *       This is primarily to allow for running microservices from within eclipse workspaces where the manifest file
425       *       is located in the project root.
426       *    <li>
427       *       Using the class loader for this class to find the file at the URL <js>"META-INF/MANIFEST.MF"</js>.
428       * </ol>
429       *
430       * @param value
431       *    The manifest file of this microservice.
432       *    <br>Can be any of the following types:
433       *    <ul>
434       *       <li>{@link ManifestFile}
435       *       <li>{@link Manifest}
436       *       <li>{@link Reader} - Containing the raw contents of the manifest.  Note that the input must end with a newline.
437       *       <li>{@link InputStream} - Containing the raw contents of the manifest.  Note that the input must end with a newline.
438       *       <li>{@link File} - File containing the raw contents of the manifest.
439       *       <li>{@link String} - Path to file containing the raw contents of the manifest.
440       *       <li>{@link Class} - Finds and loads the manifest file of the jar file that the specified class is contained within.
441       *    </ul>
442       * @return This object.
443       * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
444       */
445      public Builder manifest(Object value) throws IOException {
446         if (value == null)
447            this.manifest = null;
448         else if (value instanceof ManifestFile value2)
449            this.manifest = value2;
450         else if (value instanceof Manifest value2)
451            this.manifest = new ManifestFile(value2);
452         else if (value instanceof Reader value2)
453            this.manifest = new ManifestFile(value2);
454         else if (value instanceof InputStream value2)
455            this.manifest = new ManifestFile(value2);
456         else if (value instanceof File value2)
457            this.manifest = new ManifestFile(value2);
458         else if (value instanceof Path value2)
459            this.manifest = new ManifestFile(value2);
460         else if (value instanceof String value2)
461            this.manifest = new ManifestFile(resolveFile(value2));
462         else if (value instanceof Class value2)
463            this.manifest = new ManifestFile(value2);
464         else
465            throw rex("Invalid type passed to Builder.manifest(Object).  Type=[{0}]", cn(value));
466
467         return this;
468      }
469
470      /**
471       * Adds a bean for vars defined in the var resolver.
472       *
473       * <p>
474       * This calls {@link org.apache.juneau.commons.svl.VarResolver.Builder#bean(Class,Object)} on the var resolver used to construct the configuration
475       * object returned by {@link Microservice#getConfig()} and the var resolver returned by {@link Microservice#getVarResolver()}.
476       *
477       * @param c The bean type.
478       * @param value The bean.
479       * @param <T> The bean type.
480       * @return This object.
481       */
482      public <T> Builder varBean(Class<T> c, T value) {
483         varResolver.bean(c, value);
484         return this;
485      }
486
487      /**
488       * Augments the set of variables defined in the configuration and var resolver.
489       *
490       * <p>
491       * This calls {@link org.apache.juneau.commons.svl.VarResolver.Builder#vars(Class[])} on the var resolver used to construct the configuration
492       * object returned by {@link Microservice#getConfig()} and the var resolver returned by {@link Microservice#getVarResolver()}.
493       *
494       * @param vars The set of variables to append to the var resolver builder.
495       * @return This object.
496       */
497      @SuppressWarnings({
498         "unchecked" // Type erasure requires unchecked cast for varargs
499      })
500      public Builder vars(Class<? extends Var>...vars) {
501         varResolver.vars(vars);
502         return this;
503      }
504
505      /**
506       * Specifies the directory to use to resolve the config file and other paths defined with the config file.
507       *
508       * @param workingDir The working directory, or <jk>null</jk> to use the underlying working directory.
509       * @return This object.
510       */
511      public Builder workingDir(File workingDir) {
512         this.workingDir = workingDir;
513         return this;
514      }
515
516      /**
517       * Specifies the directory to use to resolve the config file and other paths defined with the config file.
518       *
519       * @param workingDir The working directory, or <jk>null</jk> to use the underlying working directory.
520       * @return This object.
521       */
522      public Builder workingDir(String workingDir) {
523         this.workingDir = new File(workingDir);
524         return this;
525      }
526
527      /**
528       * Registers one or more <c>@Configuration</c>-annotated classes whose <c>@Bean</c> methods/fields
529       * will be processed during microservice bootstrap.
530       *
531       * <p>
532       * Beans contributed by <c>@Configuration</c> classes are stored in the microservice's internal
533       * {@link WritableBeanStore} (accessible via {@link Microservice#getBeanStore()}).  They become
534       * available to other <c>@Bean</c> factory methods via constructor injection.
535       *
536       * <p>
537       * Explicit builder calls (e.g. {@link #config(Config)}, {@link #args(Args)}) take precedence over
538       * <c>@Bean</c>-supplied values — the resolved field is registered into the bean store <i>after</i>
539       * configurations are processed, overwriting any same-type <c>@Bean</c> contribution.
540       *
541       * @param configurations The configuration classes.  Can be <jk>null</jk> or empty.
542       * @return This object.
543       * @since 10.0.0
544       */
545      public Builder configurations(Class<?>... configurations) {
546         if (nn(configurations))
547            for (var c : configurations)
548               if (nn(c))
549                  this.configurations.add(c);
550         return this;
551      }
552
553      /**
554       * Registers one or more <c>@Configuration</c>-annotated classes.
555       *
556       * @param configurations The configuration classes.  Can be <jk>null</jk> or empty.
557       * @return This object.
558       * @see #configurations(Class...)
559       * @since 10.0.0
560       */
561      public Builder configurations(List<Class<?>> configurations) {
562         if (nn(configurations))
563            for (var c : configurations)
564               if (nn(c))
565                  this.configurations.add(c);
566         return this;
567      }
568
569      /**
570       * Specifies an external {@link WritableBeanStore} to use instead of constructing a fresh one.
571       *
572       * <p>
573       * Useful for testing and for composing a microservice into a larger application that already owns
574       * a bean store (e.g. a Spring-backed parent store).  When set, configurations supplied via
575       * {@link #configurations(Class...)} are registered into this store, and the microservice's
576       * resolved values (args, manifest, config, var resolver, listener, microservice itself) are also
577       * registered into it.
578       *
579       * @param beanStore The external bean store.  Can be <jk>null</jk> to use a fresh internal store.
580       * @return This object.
581       * @since 10.0.0
582       */
583      public Builder beanStore(WritableBeanStore beanStore) {
584         this.beanStore = beanStore;
585         return this;
586      }
587
588      /**
589       * Installs the supplied {@link BeanStore} as the {@code overridingParent} of this microservice's
590       * bean store.
591       *
592       * <p>
593       * The overlay is consulted <i>before</i> any locally-registered beans during construction-time
594       * reflective injection (see {@link BasicBeanStore#BasicBeanStore(BeanStore, BeanStore)}), so
595       * test-time overrides win over the microservice's own {@code @Bean} factory contributions.  This
596       * is the <b>Mode INJECT</b> wiring pattern for {@code Microservice}-based tests &mdash; the overlay is
597       * installed before the microservice is constructed and lives for the microservice's full lifetime.
598       * For per-test push/pop overlays against an already-started microservice, see
599       * {@link Microservice#getBeanStore()} and the push/pop primitives on
600       * {@link WritableBeanStore}.
601       *
602       * <p>
603       * Composes with {@link #beanStore(WritableBeanStore)}: when both are supplied, the supplied
604       * external store becomes the regular parent and the overlay becomes the overriding parent of
605       * a fresh wrapping {@link BasicBeanStore}.
606       *
607       * @param store The override layer.  Can be <jk>null</jk> to clear a previously-set value.
608       * @return This object.
609       * @since 10.0.0
610       */
611      @Override
612      public Builder overridingBeanStore(BeanStore store) {
613         this.overridingBeanStore = store;
614         return this;
615      }
616
617      /**
618       * Resolves the specified path.
619       *
620       * <p>
621       * If the working directory has been explicitly specified, relative paths are resolved relative to that.
622       *
623       * @param path The path to resolve.
624       * @return The resolved file.
625       */
626      protected File resolveFile(String path) {
627         if (Paths.get(path).isAbsolute())
628            return new File(path);
629         if (nn(workingDir))
630            return new File(workingDir, path);
631         return new File(path);
632      }
633   }
634
635   private static final AtomicReference<Microservice> instance = new AtomicReference<>();
636
637   /**
638    * Creates a new builder for this object.
639    *
640    * <p>
641    * Routes builder construction through {@link BeanInstantiator} so that the
642    * {@code juneau.workingDir} env default is resolved through the active
643    * {@link Settings Settings} chain.
644    *
645    * @return A new microservice builder.
646    */
647   public static Builder create() {
648      return create(BasicBeanStore.INSTANCE);
649   }
650
651   /**
652    * Creates a new builder for this object using the supplied {@link BeanStore} for
653    * {@link Value @Value}-resolution and dependency injection.
654    *
655    * @param beanStore The bean store to use for dependency injection.
656    * @return A new microservice builder.
657    */
658   public static Builder create(BeanStore beanStore) {
659      return BeanInstantiator.of(Builder.class, beanStore).run();
660   }
661
662   /**
663    * Returns the Microservice instance.
664    *
665    * <p>
666    * This method only works if there's only one Microservice instance in a JVM.
667    * Otherwise, it's just overwritten by the last instantiated microservice.
668    *
669    * @return The Microservice instance, or <jk>null</jk> if there isn't one.
670    */
671   public static Microservice getInstance() {
672      return instance.get();
673   }
674
675   private static void setInstance(Microservice m) {
676      instance.set(m);
677   }
678
679   final Messages messages = Messages.of(Microservice.class);
680
681   private final Builder builder;
682   private final Args args;
683   private final Config config;
684   private final ManifestFile manifest;
685   private final VarResolver varResolver;
686   private final Map<String,ConsoleCommand> consoleCommandMap = new ConcurrentHashMap<>();
687   private final boolean consoleEnabled;
688   private final Scanner consoleReader;
689   private final PrintWriter consoleWriter;
690   private final Thread consoleThread;
691   final File workingDir;
692   private final String configName;
693   private final WritableBeanStore beanStore;
694   private volatile boolean stopped;
695   private final ConfigPropertySource settingsSource;
696
697   private final AtomicReference<Logger> logger = new AtomicReference<>();
698
699   /**
700    * Constructor.
701    *
702    * @param builder The builder containing the settings for this microservice.
703    * @throws IOException Problem occurred reading file.
704    * @throws ParseException Malformed input encountered.
705    */
706   @SuppressWarnings({
707      "java:S3776" // Cognitive complexity acceptable for microservice initialization
708   })
709   protected Microservice(Builder builder) throws IOException, ParseException {
710      setInstance(this);
711      this.builder = builder.copy();
712      this.workingDir = builder.workingDir;
713      this.configName = builder.configName;
714
715      // --------------------------------------------------------------------------------
716      // Initialize the bean store and register any @Configuration classes.
717      // @Bean-supplied values become candidate inputs for field resolution below.
718      // Explicit builder calls always win and overwrite @Bean contributions afterward.
719      //
720      // Composition with overridingBeanStore (test-time overlay):
721      //   - no external store, no overlay  -> fresh BasicBeanStore()
722      //   - no external store, overlay set -> fresh BasicBeanStore(parent=null, overridingParent=overlay)
723      //   - external store, no overlay     -> use the external store as-is
724      //   - external store + overlay set   -> wrap with BasicBeanStore(parent=external, overridingParent=overlay)
725      // --------------------------------------------------------------------------------
726      if (nn(builder.overridingBeanStore)) {
727         this.beanStore = new BasicBeanStore(builder.beanStore, builder.overridingBeanStore);
728      } else {
729         this.beanStore = nn(builder.beanStore) ? builder.beanStore : new BasicBeanStore();
730      }
731      if (! builder.configurations.isEmpty())
732         beanStore.registerConfigurations(builder.configurations.toArray(new Class<?>[0]));
733
734      // Resolve Args: builder > @Bean > empty.
735      this.args = nn(builder.args)
736         ? builder.args
737         : beanStore.getBean(Args.class).orElseGet(() -> new Args(new String[0]));
738      beanStore.addBean(Args.class, this.args);
739
740      // --------------------------------------------------------------------------------
741      // Try to get the manifest file if it wasn't already set.
742      // --------------------------------------------------------------------------------
743      var manifest2 = builder.manifest;
744      if (manifest2 == null)
745         manifest2 = beanStore.getBean(ManifestFile.class).orElse(null);
746      if (manifest2 == null) {
747         var m = new Manifest();
748
749         // If running within an eclipse workspace, need to get it from the file system.
750         var f = resolveFile("META-INF/MANIFEST.MF");
751         if (f.exists() && f.canRead()) {
752            try (var fis = new FileInputStream(f)) {
753               m.read(fis);
754            } catch (IOException e) {
755               throw ioex(e, "Problem detected in MANIFEST.MF.  Contents below:\n{0}", read(f), e);
756            }
757         } else {
758            // Otherwise, read from manifest file in the jar file containing the main class.
759            var url = getClass().getResource("META-INF/MANIFEST.MF");
760            if (nn(url)) {
761               try {
762                  m.read(url.openStream());
763               } catch (IOException e) {
764                  throw ioex(e, "Problem detected in MANIFEST.MF.  Contents below:\n{0}", read(url.openStream()), e);
765               }
766            }
767         }
768         manifest2 = new ManifestFile(m);
769      }
770      this.manifest = manifest2;
771      beanStore.addBean(ManifestFile.class, this.manifest);
772      builder.varResolver
773         .vars(ArgsVar.create(() -> this.args))
774         .vars(ManifestFileVar.create(() -> this.manifest));
775
776      // --------------------------------------------------------------------------------
777      // Try to resolve the configuration if not specified.
778      // --------------------------------------------------------------------------------
779      var config2 = nn(builder.config) ? builder.config : beanStore.getBean(Config.class).orElse(null);
780      var configBuilder = builder.configBuilder.varResolver(builder.varResolver.build()).store(MemoryStore.DEFAULT);
781      if (config2 == null) {
782         var store = builder.configStore;
783         var cfs = workingDir == null ? FileStore.DEFAULT : FileStore.create().directory(workingDir).build();
784         boolean found = false;
785         for (var name : getCandidateConfigNames()) {
786            if (found)
787               break;
788            if (nn(store)) {
789               if (store.exists(name)) {
790                  configBuilder.store(store).name(name);
791                  found = true;
792               }
793            } else {
794               if (cfs.exists(name)) {
795                  configBuilder.store(cfs).name(name);
796                  found = true;
797               } else if (ClasspathStore.DEFAULT.exists(name)) {
798                  configBuilder.store(ClasspathStore.DEFAULT).name(name);
799                  found = true;
800               }
801            }
802         }
803         config2 = configBuilder.build();
804      }
805      this.config = config2;
806      Config.setSystemDefault(this.config);
807      this.config.addListener(this);
808      this.settingsSource = new ConfigPropertySource(this.config);
809      Settings.get().addSource(this.settingsSource);
810      beanStore.addBean(Config.class, this.config);
811      this.varResolver = builder.varResolver.bean(Config.class, config2).build();
812      beanStore.addBean(VarResolver.class, this.varResolver);
813
814      // --------------------------------------------------------------------------------
815      // Initialize console commands.
816      // --------------------------------------------------------------------------------
817      this.consoleEnabled = coalesce(builder.consoleEnabled, config.get("Console/enabled").asBoolean().orElse(false));
818      if (consoleEnabled) {
819         var c = System.console();
820         this.consoleReader = coalesce(builder.consoleReader, new Scanner(c == null ? new InputStreamReader(System.in) : c.reader()));
821         this.consoleWriter = coalesce(builder.consoleWriter, c == null ? createLoggerConsoleWriter() : c.writer());
822
823         // @Bean-supplied console commands (registered first, then overridable by builder/config below).
824         for (var cc : beanStore.getBeansOfType(ConsoleCommand.class).values())
825            consoleCommandMap.put(cc.getName(), cc);
826         for (var cc : builder.consoleCommands) {
827            consoleCommandMap.put(cc.getName(), cc);
828         }
829         for (var s : config.get("Console/commands").asStringArray().orElse(new String[0])) {
830            try {
831               var cc = (ConsoleCommand)Class.forName(s).getDeclaredConstructor().newInstance();
832               consoleCommandMap.put(cc.getName(), cc);
833            } catch (Exception e) {
834               getConsoleWriter().println("Could not create console command '" + s + "', " + localizedMessage(e));
835            }
836         }
837         consoleThread = new Thread("ConsoleThread") {
838            @Override /* Overridden from Thread */
839            public void run() {
840               var in = getConsoleReader();
841               var out = getConsoleWriter();
842
843               out.println(messages.getString("ListOfAvailableCommands"));
844               for (var cc : new TreeMap<>(getConsoleCommands()).values())
845                  out.append("\t").append(cc.getName()).append(" -- ").append(cc.getInfo()).println();
846               out.println();
847
848               while (true) {
849                  out.append("> ").flush();
850                  // Guard against empty/closed stdin: hasNextLine() returns false at end-of-stream, so
851                  // the loop ends gracefully instead of nextLine() throwing NoSuchElementException.
852                  if (! in.hasNextLine())
853                     break;
854                  var line = in.nextLine();
855                  var args2 = new Args(line);
856                  if (! args2.isEmpty())
857                     executeCommand(args2, in, out);
858               }
859            }
860         };
861         consoleThread.setDaemon(true);
862      } else {
863         this.consoleReader = null;
864         this.consoleWriter = null;
865         this.consoleThread = null;
866      }
867      // Resolve listeners.
868      // If the builder supplies an explicit listener, register it under the unnamed key (overwriting any
869      // same-key @Bean MicroserviceListener); it joins the fan-out alongside any other @Bean-supplied listeners.
870      // If, after that, no MicroserviceListener exists in the store at all, register the default
871      // BasicMicroserviceListener so the fan-out has at least one no-op participant.
872      if (nn(builder.listener))
873         beanStore.addBean(MicroserviceListener.class, builder.listener);
874      if (beanStore.getBeansOfType(MicroserviceListener.class).isEmpty())
875         beanStore.addBean(MicroserviceListener.class, new BasicMicroserviceListener());
876
877      // Self-register so @Bean factory methods can take Microservice as a constructor/method param.
878      beanStore.addBean(Microservice.class, this);
879
880      init();
881   }
882
883   /**
884    * Prints a localized message to STDERR.
885    *
886    * <p>
887    * Ignored if <js>"Console/enabled"</js> is <jk>false</jk>.
888    *
889    * @param mb The message bundle containing the message.
890    * @param messageKey The message key.
891    * @param args Optional {@link MessageFormat}-style arguments.
892    */
893   public void err(Messages mb, String messageKey, Object...args) {
894      var msg = mb.getString(messageKey, args);
895      if (consoleEnabled)
896         getLogger().warning(msg);  // NOT DEBUG
897      log(Level.SEVERE, msg);
898   }
899
900   /**
901    * Executes a console command.
902    *
903    * @param args
904    *    The command arguments.
905    *    <br>The first entry in the arguments is always the command name.
906    * @param in Console input.
907    * @param out Console output.
908    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the command returned <jk>true</jk> meaning the console thread should exit.
909    */
910   public boolean executeCommand(Args args, Scanner in, PrintWriter out) {
911      var cc = consoleCommandMap.get(args.get(0).orElse(null));
912      if (cc == null) {
913         out.println(messages.getString("UnknownCommand"));
914      } else {
915         try {
916            return cc.execute(in, out, args);
917         } catch (Exception e) {
918            e.printStackTrace(out);
919         }
920      }
921      return false;
922   }
923
924   /**
925    * Convenience method for executing a console command directly.
926    *
927    * <p>
928    * Allows you to execute a console command outside the console by simulating input and output.
929    *
930    * @param command The command name to execute.
931    * @param input Optional input to the command.  Can be <jk>null</jk>.
932    * @param args Optional command arguments to pass to the command.
933    * @return The command output.
934    */
935   public String executeCommand(String command, String input, Object...args) {
936      var sw = new StringWriter();
937      var l = list();
938      l.add(command);
939      for (var a : args)
940         l.add(s(a));
941      var args2 = new Args(l.toArray(new String[l.size()]));
942      try (var in = new Scanner(input == null ? "" : input); var out = new PrintWriter(sw)) {
943         executeCommand(args2, in, out);
944      }
945      return sw.toString();
946   }
947
948   /**
949    * Stops the console (if it's started) and calls {@link System#exit(int)}.
950    *
951    * @throws Exception Error occurred
952    */
953   public void exit() throws Exception {
954      try {
955         stopConsole();
956      } catch (Exception e) {
957         getLogger().log(Level.WARNING, "Error occurred while stopping the console.", e);
958      }
959      System.exit(0);
960   }
961
962   /**
963    * Returns the command-line arguments passed into the application.
964    *
965    * <p>
966    * This method can be called from the class constructor.
967    *
968    * <p>
969    * See {@link Args} for details on using this method.
970    *
971    * @return The command-line arguments passed into the application.
972    */
973   public Args getArgs() { return args; }
974
975   /**
976    * Returns the external INI-style configuration file that can be used to configure your microservice.
977    *
978    * <p>
979    * The config location is determined in the following order:
980    * <ol class='spaced-list'>
981    *    <li>
982    *       The first argument passed to the microservice jar.
983    *    <li>
984    *       The <c>Main-Config</c> entry in the microservice jar manifest file.
985    *    <li>
986    *       The name of the microservice jar with a <js>".cfg"</js> suffix (e.g.
987    *       <js>"mymicroservice.jar"</js>-&gt;<js>"mymicroservice.cfg"</js>).
988    * </ol>
989    *
990    * <p>
991    * If all methods for locating the config fail, then this method returns an empty config.
992    *
993    * <p>
994    * Subclasses can set their own config file by using the following methods:
995    * <ul class='javatree'>
996    *    <li class='jm'>{@link Builder#configStore(ConfigStore)}
997    *    <li class='jm'>{@link Builder#configName(String)}
998    * </ul>
999    *
1000    * <p>
1001    * String variables are automatically resolved using the variable resolver returned by {@link #getVarResolver()}.
1002    *
1003    * <p>
1004    * This method can be called from the class constructor.
1005    *
1006    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1007    * <p class='bini'>
1008    *    <cc>#--------------------------</cc>
1009    *    <cc># My section</cc>
1010    *    <cc>#--------------------------</cc>
1011    *    <cs>[MySection]</cs>
1012    *
1013    *    <cc># An integer</cc>
1014    *    <ck>anInt</ck> = 1
1015    *
1016    *    <cc># A boolean</cc>
1017    *    <ck>aBoolean</ck> = true
1018    *
1019    *    <cc># An int array</cc>
1020    *    <ck>anIntArray</ck> = 1,2,3
1021    *
1022    *    <cc># A POJO that can be converted from a String</cc>
1023    *    <ck>aURL</ck> = http://foo
1024    *
1025    *    <cc># A POJO that can be converted from JSON</cc>
1026    *    <ck>aBean</ck> = {foo:'bar',baz:123}
1027    *
1028    *    <cc># A system property</cc>
1029    *    <ck>locale</ck> = $S{java.locale, en_US}
1030    *
1031    *    <cc># An environment variable</cc>
1032    *    <ck>path</ck> = $E{PATH, unknown}
1033    *
1034    *    <cc># A manifest file entry</cc>
1035    *    <ck>mainClass</ck> = $MF{Main-Class}
1036    *
1037    *    <cc># Another value in this config file</cc>
1038    *    <ck>sameAsAnInt</ck> = $C{MySection/anInt}
1039    *
1040    *    <cc># A command-line argument in the form "myarg=foo"</cc>
1041    *    <ck>myArg</ck> = $A{myarg}
1042    *
1043    *    <cc># The first command-line argument</cc>
1044    *    <ck>firstArg</ck> = $A{0}
1045    *
1046    *    <cc># Look for system property, or env var if that doesn't exist, or command-line arg if that doesn't exist.</cc>
1047    *    <ck>nested</ck> = $S{mySystemProperty,$E{MY_ENV_VAR,$A{0}}}
1048    *
1049    *    <cc># A POJO with embedded variables</cc>
1050    *    <ck>aBean2</ck> = {foo:'$A{0}',baz:$C{MySection/anInt}}
1051    * </p>
1052    *
1053    * <p class='bjava'>
1054    *    <jc>// Java code for accessing config entries above.</jc>
1055    *    Config <jv>config</jv> = getConfig();
1056    *
1057    *    <jk>int</jk> <jv>anInt</jv> = <jv>config</jv>.get(<js>"MySection/anInt"</js>).asInteger().orElse(-1);
1058    *    <jk>boolean</jk> <jv>aBoolean</jv> = <jv>config</jv>.get(<js>"MySection/aBoolean"</js>).asBoolean().orElse(<jk>false</jk>);
1059    *    <jk>int</jk>[] <jv>anIntArray</jv> = <jv>config</jv>.get(<js>"MySection/anIntArray"</js>).as(<jk>int</jk>[].<jk>class</jk>).orElse(<jk>null</jk>);
1060    *    URL <jv>aURL</jv> = <jv>config</jv>.get(<js>"MySection/aURL"</js>).as(URL.<jk>class</jk>).orElse(<jk>null</jk>);
1061    *    MyBean <jv>aBean</jv> = <jv>config</jv>.get(<js>"MySection/aBean"</js>).as(MyBean.<jk>class</jk>).orElse(<jk>null</jk>);
1062    *    Locale <jv>locale</jv> = <jv>config</jv>.get(<js>"MySection/locale"</js>).as(Locale.<jk>class</jk>).orElse(<jk>null</jk>);
1063    *    String <jv>path</jv> = <jv>config</jv>.get(<js>"MySection/path"</js>).orElse(<jk>null</jk>);
1064    *    String <jv>mainClass</jv> = <jv>config</jv>.get(<js>"MySection/mainClass"</js>).orElse(<jk>null</jk>);
1065    *    <jk>int</jk> <jv>sameAsAnInt</jv> = <jv>config</jv>.get(<js>"MySection/sameAsAnInt"</js>).asInteger().orElse(<jk>null</jk>);
1066    *    String <jv>myArg</jv> = <jv>config</jv>.getString(<js>"MySection/myArg"</js>);
1067    *    String <jv>firstArg</jv> = <jv>config</jv>.getString(<js>"MySection/firstArg"</js>);
1068    * </p>
1069    *
1070    * @return The config file for this application, or <jk>null</jk> if no config file is configured.
1071    */
1072   public Config getConfig() { return config; }
1073
1074   /**
1075    * Returns the console commands associated with this microservice.
1076    *
1077    * @return The console commands associated with this microservice as an unmodifiable map.
1078    */
1079   public final Map<String,ConsoleCommand> getConsoleCommands() { return consoleCommandMap; }
1080
1081   /**
1082    * Returns the logger for this microservice.
1083    *
1084    * @return The logger for this microservice.
1085    */
1086   public Logger getLogger() { return logger.get(); }
1087
1088   /**
1089    * Returns the internal {@link WritableBeanStore} that backs this microservice's inject-aware bootstrap.
1090    *
1091    * <p>
1092    * The store is populated with this microservice's resolved values (<c>Args</c>, <c>ManifestFile</c>,
1093    * <c>Config</c>, <c>VarResolver</c>, <c>MicroserviceListener</c>, and <c>Microservice</c> itself),
1094    * plus any beans contributed by <c>@Configuration</c> classes registered via
1095    * {@link Builder#configurations(Class...)}.
1096    *
1097    * <p>
1098    * Lifecycle integration beans (e.g. <c>JettyServerComponent</c> contributed by <c>JettyConfiguration</c>)
1099    * implement {@link MicroserviceListener} and participate in the {@link #start()} / {@link #stop()}
1100    * fan-out.  External callers can query the store directly to look up beans contributed by user-supplied
1101    * <c>@Configuration</c> classes.
1102    *
1103    * <p>
1104    * The store is closed during {@link #stop()}, which triggers <c>@PreDestroy</c> hooks on resolved
1105    * beans.
1106    *
1107    * @return The microservice's bean store.  Never <jk>null</jk>.
1108    * @since 10.0.0
1109    */
1110   public WritableBeanStore getBeanStore() { return beanStore; }
1111
1112   /**
1113    * Returns the main jar manifest file contents as a simple {@link JsonMap}.
1114    *
1115    * <p>
1116    * This map consists of the contents of {@link Manifest#getMainAttributes()} with the keys and entries converted to
1117    * simple strings.
1118    * <p>
1119    * This method can be called from the class constructor.
1120    *
1121    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1122    * <p class='bjava'>
1123    *    <jc>// Get Main-Class from manifest file.</jc>
1124    *    String <jv>mainClass</jv> = Microservice.<jsm>getManifest</jsm>().getString(<js>"Main-Class"</js>, <js>"unknown"</js>);
1125    *
1126    *    <jc>// Get Rest-Resources from manifest file.</jc>
1127    *    String[] <jv>restResources</jv> = Microservice.<jsm>getManifest</jsm>().getStringArray(<js>"Rest-Resources"</js>);
1128    * </p>
1129    *
1130    * @return The manifest file from the main jar, or <jk>null</jk> if the manifest file could not be retrieved.
1131    */
1132   public ManifestFile getManifest() { return manifest; }
1133
1134   /**
1135    * Returns the variable resolver for resolving variables in strings and files.
1136    *
1137    * <p>
1138    * Variables can be controlled by the following methods:
1139    * <ul class='javatree'>
1140    *    <li class='jm'>{@link Builder#vars(Class...)}
1141    *    <li class='jm'>{@link Builder#varBean(Class,Object)}
1142    * </ul>
1143    *
1144    * @return The VarResolver used by this Microservice, or <jk>null</jk> if it was never created.
1145    */
1146   public VarResolver getVarResolver() { return varResolver; }
1147
1148   /**
1149    * Initializes this microservice.
1150    *
1151    * <p>
1152    * This method can be called whenever the microservice is not started.
1153    *
1154    * <p>
1155    * It will initialize (or reinitialize) the console commands, system properties, and logger.
1156    *
1157    * @return This object.
1158    * @throws ParseException Malformed input encountered.
1159    * @throws IOException Couldn't read a file.
1160    */
1161   public synchronized Microservice init() throws IOException, ParseException {
1162
1163      // --------------------------------------------------------------------------------
1164      // Set system properties.
1165      // --------------------------------------------------------------------------------
1166      var spKeys = config.getKeys("SystemProperties");
1167      if (nn(spKeys))
1168         for (var key : spKeys)
1169            System.setProperty(key, config.get("SystemProperties/" + key).orElse(null));
1170
1171      // --------------------------------------------------------------------------------
1172      // Initialize logging.
1173      // --------------------------------------------------------------------------------
1174      logger.set(builder.logger);
1175      var logConfig = nn(builder.logConfig) ? builder.logConfig : new LogConfig();
1176      if (logger.get() == null) {
1177         LogManager.getLogManager().reset();
1178         logger.set(Logger.getLogger(""));
1179         var logFile = coalesce(logConfig.logFile, config.get("Logging/logFile").orElse(null));
1180
1181         if (ine(logFile)) {
1182            var logDir = coalesce(logConfig.logDir, config.get("Logging/logDir").orElse("."));
1183            var logDirFile = resolveFile(logDir);
1184            mkdirs(logDirFile, false);
1185            logDir = logDirFile.getAbsolutePath();
1186            System.setProperty("juneau.logDir", logDir);
1187
1188            var append = coalesce(logConfig.append, config.get("Logging/append").asBoolean().orElse(false));
1189            var limit = coalesce(logConfig.limit, config.get("Logging/limit").asInteger().orElse(1024 * 1024));
1190            var count = coalesce(logConfig.count, config.get("Logging/count").asInteger().orElse(1));
1191
1192            var fh = new FileHandler(logDir + '/' + logFile, limit, count, append);
1193
1194            var f = logConfig.formatter;
1195            if (f == null) {
1196               var format = config.get("Logging/format").orElse("[{date} {level}] {msg}%n");
1197               var dateFormat = config.get("Logging/dateFormat").orElse("yyyy.MM.dd hh:mm:ss");
1198               var useStackTraceHashes = config.get("Logging/useStackTraceHashes").asBoolean().orElse(false);
1199               f = new LogEntryFormatter(format, dateFormat, useStackTraceHashes);
1200            }
1201            fh.setFormatter(f);
1202            fh.setLevel(coalesce(logConfig.fileLevel, config.get("Logging/fileLevel").as(Level.class).orElse(Level.INFO)));
1203            logger.get().addHandler(fh);
1204
1205            var ch = new ConsoleHandler();
1206            ch.setLevel(coalesce(logConfig.consoleLevel, config.get("Logging/consoleLevel").as(Level.class).orElse(Level.WARNING)));
1207            ch.setFormatter(f);
1208            logger.get().addHandler(ch);
1209         }
1210      }
1211
1212      var loggerLevels = config.get("Logging/levels").as(JsonMap.class).orElseGet(JsonMap::new);
1213      for (var l : loggerLevels.keySet())
1214         Logger.getLogger(l).setLevel(loggerLevels.get(l, Level.class));
1215      for (var l : logConfig.levels.keySet())
1216         Logger.getLogger(l).setLevel(logConfig.levels.get(l));
1217
1218      return this;
1219   }
1220
1221   /**
1222    * Joins the application with the current thread.
1223    *
1224    * <p>
1225    * Default implementation is a no-op.
1226    *
1227    * @return This object.
1228    * @throws Exception Error occurred
1229    */
1230   @SuppressWarnings({
1231      "java:S112" // throws Exception intentional - callback/lifecycle method
1232   })
1233   public Microservice join() throws Exception {
1234      return this;
1235   }
1236
1237   /**
1238    * Kill the JVM by calling <c>System.exit(2);</c>.
1239    */
1240   public void kill() {
1241      // This triggers the shutdown hook.
1242      System.exit(2);
1243   }
1244
1245   @Override /* Overridden from ConfigChangeListener */
1246   public void onConfigChange(ConfigEvents events) {
1247      for (var l : beanStore.getBeansOfType(MicroserviceListener.class).values())
1248         l.onConfigChange(this, events);
1249   }
1250
1251   /**
1252    * Prints a localized message to the console writer.
1253    *
1254    * <p>
1255    * Ignored if <js>"Console/enabled"</js> is <jk>false</jk>.
1256    *
1257    * @param mb The message bundle containing the message.
1258    * @param messageKey The message key.
1259    * @param args Optional {@link MessageFormat}-style arguments.
1260    */
1261   public void out(Messages mb, String messageKey, Object...args) {
1262      var msg = mb.getString(messageKey, args);
1263      if (consoleEnabled)
1264         getConsoleWriter().println(msg);
1265      log(Level.INFO, msg);
1266   }
1267
1268   /**
1269    * Start this application.
1270    *
1271    * <p>
1272    * Iterates over every {@link MicroserviceListener} bean in the {@linkplain #getBeanStore() bean store} (in
1273    * registration order) and calls {@link MicroserviceListener#onStart(Microservice)} on each.
1274    *
1275    * @return This object.
1276    * @throws Exception Error occurred.
1277    */
1278   @SuppressWarnings({
1279      "java:S112" // throws Exception intentional - callback/lifecycle method
1280   })
1281   public synchronized Microservice start() throws Exception {
1282
1283      if (config.getName() == null)
1284         err(messages, "RunningClassWithoutConfig", cns(this));
1285      else
1286         out(messages, "RunningClassWithConfig", cns(this), config.getName());
1287
1288      Runtime.getRuntime().addShutdownHook(new Thread("ShutdownHookThread") {
1289         @Override /* Overridden from Thread */
1290         public void run() {
1291            try {
1292               Microservice.this.stop();
1293               Microservice.this.stopConsole();
1294            } catch (Exception e) {
1295               getLogger().log(Level.WARNING, "Error occurred during microservice shutdown.", e);
1296            }
1297         }
1298      });
1299
1300      for (var l : beanStore.getBeansOfType(MicroserviceListener.class).values())
1301         l.onStart(this);
1302
1303      return this;
1304   }
1305
1306   /**
1307    * Starts the console thread for this microservice.
1308    *
1309    * @return This object.
1310    * @throws Exception Error occurred
1311    */
1312   @SuppressWarnings({
1313      "java:S112" // throws Exception intentional - callback/lifecycle method
1314   })
1315   public synchronized Microservice startConsole() throws Exception {
1316      if (nn(consoleThread) && ! consoleThread.isAlive())
1317         consoleThread.start();
1318      return this;
1319   }
1320
1321   /**
1322    * Stop this application.
1323    *
1324    * <p>
1325    * Iterates over every {@link MicroserviceListener} bean in the {@linkplain #getBeanStore() bean store} in
1326    * <i>reverse</i> registration order and calls {@link MicroserviceListener#onStop(Microservice)} on each, then
1327    * closes the bean store (firing any {@code @PreDestroy} hooks).
1328    *
1329    * @return This object.
1330    * @throws Exception Error occurred
1331    */
1332   @SuppressWarnings({
1333      "java:S112" // throws Exception intentional - callback/lifecycle method
1334   })
1335   public synchronized Microservice stop() throws Exception {
1336      if (stopped)
1337         return this;
1338      stopped = true;
1339      if (nn(settingsSource))
1340         Settings.get().removeSource(settingsSource);
1341      // Detach this microservice from JVM-global config state.  The config store (MemoryStore.DEFAULT) and
1342      // the system-default config slot are process-wide shared statics; leaving a stopped microservice
1343      // registered as a config-change listener there causes its onConfigChange() to fire against an
1344      // already-closed bean store when an unrelated component later mutates the shared config.
1345      if (nn(config)) {
1346         config.removeListener(this);
1347         if (Config.getSystemDefault() == config)
1348            Config.setSystemDefault(null);
1349      }
1350      var listeners = new ArrayList<>(beanStore.getBeansOfType(MicroserviceListener.class).values());
1351      Collections.reverse(listeners);
1352      for (var l : listeners)
1353         l.onStop(this);
1354      try {
1355         beanStore.close();
1356      } catch (Exception e) {
1357         // @PreDestroy errors should not prevent the rest of the shutdown sequence; surface via logger.
1358         var lg = getLogger();
1359         if (nn(lg))
1360            lg.log(Level.WARNING, localizedMessage(e), e);
1361      }
1362      return this;
1363   }
1364
1365   /**
1366    * Stops the console thread for this microservice.
1367    *
1368    * @return This object.
1369    * @throws Exception Error occurred
1370    */
1371   @SuppressWarnings({
1372      "java:S112" // throws Exception intentional - callback/lifecycle method
1373   })
1374   public synchronized Microservice stopConsole() throws Exception {
1375      if (nn(consoleThread) && consoleThread.isAlive())
1376         consoleThread.interrupt();
1377      return this;
1378   }
1379
1380   private List<String> getCandidateConfigNames() {
1381      if (nn(configName))
1382         return Collections.singletonList(configName);
1383
1384      var args2 = getArgs();
1385      var configFile = args2.get("configFile");
1386      if (configFile.isPresent())
1387         return Collections.singletonList(configFile.get());
1388
1389      var mainConfig = getManifest().get("Main-Config");
1390      if (mainConfig.isPresent())
1391         return Collections.singletonList(mainConfig.get());
1392
1393      return Config.getCandidateSystemDefaultConfigNames();
1394   }
1395
1396   /**
1397    * Returns the console reader.
1398    *
1399    * <p>
1400    * Subclasses can override this method to provide their own console input.
1401    *
1402    * @return The console reader.  Never <jk>null</jk>.
1403    */
1404   protected Scanner getConsoleReader() { return consoleReader; }
1405
1406   /**
1407    * Returns the console writer.
1408    *
1409    * <p>
1410    * Subclasses can override this method to provide their own console output.
1411    *
1412    * @return The console writer.  Never <jk>null</jk>.
1413    */
1414   protected PrintWriter getConsoleWriter() { return consoleWriter; }
1415
1416   private PrintWriter createLoggerConsoleWriter() {
1417      var fallbackLogger = coalesce(getLogger(), Logger.getLogger(cn(this)));
1418      return new PrintWriter(new Writer() {
1419         private final StringBuilder buffer = new StringBuilder();
1420         @Override
1421         public void write(char[] cbuf, int off, int len) throws IOException {
1422            for (var i = off; i < off + len; i++) {
1423               var ch = cbuf[i];
1424               if (ch == '\n') {
1425                  flushBuffer();
1426               } else if (ch != '\r') {
1427                  buffer.append(ch);
1428               }
1429            }
1430         }
1431         @Override
1432         public void flush() throws IOException {
1433            flushBuffer();
1434         }
1435         @Override
1436         public void close() throws IOException {
1437            flushBuffer();
1438         }
1439         private void flushBuffer() {
1440            if (! buffer.isEmpty()) {
1441               fallbackLogger.info(buffer.toString());
1442               buffer.setLength(0);
1443            }
1444         }
1445      }, true);
1446   }
1447
1448   /**
1449    * Logs a message to the log file.
1450    *
1451    * @param level The log level.
1452    * @param message The message text.
1453    * @param args Optional {@link MessageFormat}-style arguments.
1454    */
1455   protected void log(Level level, String message, Object...args) {
1456      var msg = args.length == 0 ? message : f(message, args);
1457      getLogger().log(level, msg);
1458   }
1459
1460   /**
1461    * Resolves the specified path.
1462    *
1463    * <p>
1464    * If the working directory has been explicitly specified, relative paths are resolved relative to that.
1465    *
1466    * @param path The path to resolve.
1467    * @return The resolved path.
1468    */
1469   protected File resolveFile(String path) {
1470      if (Paths.get(path).isAbsolute())
1471         return new File(path);
1472      if (nn(workingDir))
1473         return new File(workingDir, path);
1474      return new File(path);
1475   }
1476}
